history.msg: Captured 5 resources (source=human, all primary) — adr.github.io, AWS ADR guide, Chip Huyen AI Engineering, cognitect.com Nygard ADR original, martinfowler.com scaling-architecture-conversationally
Corpus generated — standard depth (suggested), diagram=true, ~2900 words. 7 concepts introduced. All 5 primary resources cited. ADR format (5 sections), synthesis of 14.4+14.7+14.9. No forward refs.

Corpus critic PASSED cycle 1 — all 6 checks green. ~2390w (soft: slightly below 2500 floor, non-blocking). All 5 primaries cited (>85% claim coverage). No forward refs. Scope clean. Beginner-readable.

HITL #2 complete — all 9 sections approved as written, depth=standard, diagram=true (flowchart: decision-matrix inputs → tier → 5 ADR sections), expected_delivery=7min. Soft: 110w below 2500 floor, accepted.

Diagram critic PASSED cycle 1 — 9-node flowchart TD: 3 decision-matrix inputs (blue) → Chosen Tier (green) → 5 ADR sections in order (dark blue). PNG rendered 1400px transparent bg. Soft: 9 nodes vs 3-8 guideline, accepted (pedagogical requirement).

Reading generated — 1310w, 7min. All 5 primary resources cited. Diagram embedded. 6 fixed sections, no frontmatter. Quiz generated — 5 MCQ, positions [1,3,0,2,1], max_share=0.40, bias_defense_applied=true. All 5 LOs covered.
Reading critic PASSED cycle 1 — 1310w in [1200,1400], all 5 primaries cited, 6 fixed sections, no frontmatter, diagram embedded, scope clean, no forward refs, beginner-readable.
Quiz critic PASSED cycle 2 (cycle 1 fail: length_bias 0.60, q1+q3+q5 fixed). 5 MCQ, max_share=0.40, rate=0.00, 0 absurd distractors, all 5 LOs covered.
All artifact critics passed — advancing to HITL #3.
HITL #3 approved — reading 1310w/7min PASS (cycle 1), quiz 5MCQ bias-defended PASS (cycle 2, length-bias fixed), diagram 9-node TD PASS (cycle 1). No carry-over failures.
